## Cinched
- Pure form migration: the motif and dimension terms are unchanged; only the `axl_voices` keyword is dropped and the motif promoted to lead.
- Out of scope: any motif rename or re-voicing — touch the form, nothing else.
## Done when
`grep -rn "//axl_voices" Tools/` returns zero annotation lines;
the current form carries every voicing the legacy form did;

AXLA's description of the axl_voices-leading form is reduced to a historical note since nothing authors it — optionally backed by a cold-probe/validator check that rejects the leading form to prevent regression;
and the guidance restatements sweep in the same act — claude-cmk-core.md's read-only-legacy sentences (and MCM's Annotation description if it carries the form) reduce to the same historical note.
## Character
Cross-cutting migration; staged, judgment lives in the triage.
## Done when

The AXLA mutability-phase trichotomy moves from advisory toward declared: existing axr_record voicings repo-wide are retrofitted to declare exactly one phase (axd_immutable / axd_petrify / axd_mutable),
and the phase requirement is banked as an enforcement obligation at the matricula validator home (VOSMM's validator inventory), so the future validator enforces what this retrofit establishes.
First retrofit targets are the JJK gallops family (gallops, heat, pace, tack — axd_mutable, per their members' in-place updates).
## Cinched
The rule and its staged-enforcement clause are already stated at axr_record and the Mutability Dimensions subsection (commit b2799a72e); this pace executes the voicing rollout that clause defers.
The type-scheme linter AXLA anticipates is a forward reference to the matricula validator — it does not exist yet and is not built here; enforcement tightening is banked, not implemented.
Retrofit is incremental — coverage grows pace by pace, never a single big-bang sweep.

Specification authoring; concept-tier judgment, not mechanical.
## Done when

A vo subdoc exists — VLS extension or a new vo census spec, pick the file at mount — homing the Matricula entity nature (the transient raise→freeze→query→discard census), the four-tier decomposition, and the MVP method set: the scan, the two seating-invariant validators (terminal-exclusivity, exact collision), and the report render plus acronym-index generation.

The subdoc cites CMK/MCM as the doctrine home for the minting rules rather than restating them, and points to ₣Bj for the deferred widening (CHECK / LIST / TRACE, SAFE-REPREFIX, the engagement determinations, gestalt labels).

Parts reuse the existing VLS quoins (Inscription, Signet, Vesture, Cipher, Epithet); the subdoc mints only the three new vo quoins it needs — Residue, Finding, and a register-aligned freeze verb — each through the grep gate and MCM Lapidary.
## Cinched

See the paddock for full shape: VOK standalone-binary placement (source veiled, binary gitignored in target), CMK-cited doctrine, tabtarget-only MVP, the freeze/builder object pattern, mechanical-never-linguistic, and the structural-secrecy boundary.
## Character

## Done when

VMS-VoxMatriculaSpec.adoc is reflowed to the MCM heavy line-break style its siblings VLS/VOS0/AXLA use — linked terms broken onto their own lines per MCM Term Integration Patterns — for diff and blame granularity.
Line-break only: every word, marker, and attribute-reference preserved; lands as its own commit.
## Cinched
The .adoc house rule is MCM line-break discipline, not the gazette semantic-linefeed rule (which governs paddocks and dockets only).
## Character
Design decision; high-stakes naming-system architecture, resolved at mount through judgment.
Gates the build-out pace — VMS cannot be authored to final form until this is cinched.
## Done when
The vo-family gestalt direction is decided and cinched, answering:
whether to re-gestalt the `vo` cipher around the Vox naming-system whole;
how Liturgical / Obscura / Matricula relate — peers, or an umbrella plus branches;
where VLS belongs — a branch of Vox, or a foundation beneath it and beneath BUK/JJK;
and how much of the Matricula method VMS may document, given the secrecy gradient.
If the resolution implies a large vo-family restructure, that execution may spawn its own paces or tilt to ₣Bj — a mount-time call, not pre-decided here.
## Findings to resolve against
Discovered in the slate conversation; homeless until this pace cinches them.
`vo` is ALREADY one cipher — fossil-named "Vox Obscura" in `Tools/vok/vof/src/vofc_registry.rs`, after its first branch.
VLS (`vosl*`), Obscura (`voso*`), and Matricula (`vosm*`) already share it.

Cipher and distribution are orthogonal axes.

The Matricula's secrecy rests on the four paddock guards (uncollected, veiled, unlinked, gitignored), never on the cipher — earlier "secrecy rests on vo-not-shipping" was a fragile-base-class flaw, retracted.
`vof` is the shared seam: linked by `vvr`/`vvx` (ships) and depended on by the Matricula.
It cannot move into the never-shipped Matricula executable; the Matricula depends on it, never contains it.
Secrecy gradient: the binary is gitignore-strength (never travels); VMS is veil-strength (committed, travels to the test fleet — cerebro, rocket).
"Keep it on the machine" holds for the code but not for the method-as-documented — hence the threat-model call on how much method VMS writes down versus leaves to the gitignored crate.
VLS placement is open on cognitive grounds only — no distribution constraint forces it either way; AXL is prior art for the separate-vocabulary-anchor option.

## Census mechanism
Re-derive the census at mount; do not trust any prior enumeration — sheaf contents shift.
Derive the sheaf set from the codex's own `include::` directives (check included sheaves for nested `include::` lines too),
then grep `^\[\[` across that sheaf set, the codex itself excluded.
Classify each anchor found:
- anchor name is an `RBr_` rivet ID → rivet, lawful at its constraint site: leave.
- anchor followed by an `{attribute}::` definition-list opener (an `//ax...` annotation line may sit between) → definition site: IN SCOPE, repatriate.
- anchor followed by a section heading or anything else → section anchor: leave.
## Move rule
For each in-scope site, relocate the whole definition block — the `[[anchor]]` line, any annotation line, the `{attribute}::` line, and the definition body — into the codex:
place it in the codex section that already defines sibling quoins of the same prefix family;
if the family has no codex definition block, land it inside the codex section that wraps that sheaf's `include::`, above the include line.
The mapping section needs no edits — `<<anchor>>` targets resolve pandect-wide.
Heal each sheaf where a block left:
surrounding prose keeps citing the quoin by attribute reference and must still read as continuous narrative;
never leave a restated definition body behind (one home, per ACG allocation discipline).
## Done when
The census grep, re-run, shows zero in-scope definition sites outside the codex;
a scripted check confirms every `:attr:` mapping target in the codex resolves to exactly one `[[anchor]]` across the codex plus its included sheaves;
each touched sheaf reads coherently at its excision points.

Repair the confirmation-gate prompt so it renders on the non-interactive dispatch path.
Characterized live 260711 on cerebro against the escheat gate, so no further reproduction is owed.
bud_dispatch's non-interactive branch relays tabtarget output to the terminal through a line-oriented `while IFS= read -r` loop, which cannot forward a partial line.
buc_require's prompt carries no trailing newline — the answer is meant to be typed beside it — so the relay holds those bytes while the verb blocks on `read </dev/tty`:
the operator sees a DANGER banner, no prompt, and a hung process.
Platform-independent, not Linux-specific:
the handful of BURD_INTERACTIVE tabtargets take the `tee` branch and render correctly (rbw-gPI proves it),
while every other confirm-gated verb — escheat, depot-unmake, marshal-zero, manor-raze — is broken on every platform.
Repair: keep the danger banner and its prompt on one ordered stream by newline-terminating the prompt through stderr.
Do NOT move the prompt to /dev/tty (buc_countdown's channel) — that races the relay and can print the prompt above its own danger banner.
Sweep the sibling partial-line prompts in buh_handbook's interactive read helpers, which carry the same latent defect.

## Docket
Implement the relay-then-read chain-lifetime law.
Study findings (260712, this session): no code ever deletes a fact — consumption was never destructive;
a fact's life is bounded solely by the dispatcher's current/→previous/ promotion (rm -rf at depth 2);
buf_relay exists, is self-tested, and has zero production callers — RBr_3e7 is the only thing forbidding it;
no existing test asserts the old no-relay semantics, so the change is rivet + relay calls + spec + net-new tests.
The law: every chain consumer calls buf_relay first, then the express-or-chain elect;
a fact survives any run of consecutive chain verbs and dies at the first non-chain dispatch;
a fact is replaced by the next same-name wellhead write (natural supersession via promotion);
failed-consumer retry falls out free — relay precedes the failure point, so a failed consumer has already passed the baton to its own retry (the dispatcher's documented fail-after-forward property).

## Transport discipline (earned the hard way; memo §4/§9)
Beast's default ssh shell is cmd.exe, so `cmd` sees the command line before PowerShell does.
Never put `|` inside a quoted `powershell -Command` string over ssh — cmd tokenizes it as a pipe;
base64 the script and use `-EncodedCommand`
(`printf '%s' "$PS" | iconv -f UTF-8 -t UTF-16LE | openssl enc -base64 -A`).
Quote every path containing a space.
When handing PowerShell to the operator to paste at the console, keep every line under ~100 characters —
conhost hard-wraps a longer pasted line into two commands — and send exactly one code block per message, with no prose between blocks.
Never read `$LASTEXITCODE` across a command that may not have launched.
Verify external facts (release tags, asset names, sizes) from the curia before scripting against them.

## Docket
Rule on whether the release process is safe, sound, and simple enough to be robust — and if it is not, say what it should be instead.

Read what you need, starting from `RELEASE.md` (the outer procedure), the cut ceremony it delegates to (`.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`), the marshal verbs (`Tools/rbk/rblm_*`), the release fixtures (pyx, loupe, damnatio in the theurge crate), this heat's paddock, and the dockets of the paces that follow this one.
The evidence is the symptom set, all of it real, all of it from the 260713 cut and its predecessors:

a candidate reached the remote carrying the entire private repository in the tip's parent commit, green on every gate this project owns, because every gate reads the tip tree and none reads history — the operator caught it by noticing a number looked strange, and it landed in a private repo by luck of remote wiring;
a fixture meaningful only pre-strip was made a member of the credless suite that ships, handing every consumer a red test asserting the absence of documents they never had;
the generated, shipped tabtarget table documented five withheld commands;
a typed-confirmation guard proved worthless because the agent driving the ceremony skips confirmations;
marshal zero blanks whatever tree it stands in and nearly blanked the working one;
and three ceremony steps were false or unrunnable as literally written.
Every one of those was answered by adding a gate, a fixture, or a paragraph to a document an agent obeys as authority — the document is now some 450 lines and three of its steps were wrong.
The charge is to interrogate that pattern, not extend it.
Chief question, to evaluate rather than assume: the process is strip-by-subtraction — begin with everything, remove what is private — and every leak class above descends from that.

Weigh build-by-addition — construct the candidate from an explicit manifest of what ships, so no withheld blob ever enters the candidate's object graph — against it, on safety AND simplicity, and name what it would cost.
Look for structural simplifications nobody here has seen.
The ruling must say what to DELETE, not only what to add.

Destructive acts stay in operator hands; the ceremony may check, never destroy.
The process is agent-driven with an operator gate at every step — a guard an agent can skip is not a guard.
This pace produces a ruling, not code — but the ruling is binding on the paces that follow it, and it must say explicitly which of them survive, which are reshaped, and which are void.
## Done when
A recommended design stands, stated as a decision, with the minimal invariants a candidate must satisfy and a verdict on every current gate — load-bearing or theater;
each following pace is declared surviving, reshaped, or void, and the reshaped ones are reslated out of the ruling rather than left to inertia;
and the strongest argument against the recommendation is recorded beside it.

The defect, diagnosed live in `Tools/rbk/rblm_perambulation.sh`:
data born and consumed inside one program is flattened into an encoded string
(`"prefix|verdict"` rows) and re-parsed at every use site (`${z_row%%|*}` / `${z_row#*|}`),
when a structural record was available — an encode/decode pair with no boundary.
The decode is where the failures live:
a row missing its delimiter decodes *successfully* into garbage (silent misjudgment),
the delimiter's legality in the payload is a constraint recorded nowhere,
and the field shape exists only inside duplicated parse expressions.
Language-independent; the training-corpus habit of delimited-string tables is why it recurs.

2. Tree-identity refusal: run `tt/rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh not-this-tree` — a DELIBERATELY WRONG basename, never the real one; zero must never be given the true basename — expect the naming refusal.
3. Feign refusal: run `tt/rbw-MF.MarshalFeigns.sh` in the working repository on `main` — expect the branch-guard die ("Refusing to feign on 'main'").

4. The cut: run `tt/rbw-ME.MarshalExpedes.sh` against a nonexistent absolute path, sibling of the working repository (convention `rbm_candidate_scratch`), as a plain tool call with NO environment override — expect it to run to its verdict: one commit, both sweeps clean.
Push nothing; delete the scratch target directory afterward.
If the clone step fails because the quarantine remote is gone, stop and surface — the remote is operator-managed.
Read outcomes from `../logs-buk/` per tabtarget discipline.
## Done when
Both prompts are gone and zero's and lustrate's remain;
expede completes a cut from a tool call with no environment override;
and all three refusal checks fired as refusals.
